result:
**Goal: Add a Percolation Theory & Critical Phenomena mode.**

Percolation is the foundational model of statistical physics that's conspicuously absent from this 153-mode collection. It demonstrates the sharpest, most visually dramatic phase transition in all of complexity science — a grid goes from disconnected islands to a single spanning cluster at a razor-thin critical threshold (p_c ≈ 0.5927 for site percolation). The mode would feature real-time cluster identification with distinct coloring, live order-parameter tracking (spanning probability, largest cluster fraction), fractal geometry at criticality, and presets covering **Site Percolation, Bond Percolation, Invasion Percolation, Directed Percolation, Bootstrap Percolation, and Continuum Percolation**. It connects conceptually to several existing modes (forest fire *is* a percolation problem, SIR epidemic spreading, sandpile criticality) while providing the clean, canonical version that ties them all together.

Want me to build it?
